ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic cookie class; no specific veggie-variant cookie entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 category A00LC covers biscuits and cookies broadly; no specific vegetable-enriched cookie sub-entry exists in the published hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for vegetable-enriched cookie identified in the clinical terminology; null applied honestly.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one cookie weighing approximately 30 g, providing about 16.5 kcal, 3.2 g protein, and 0.7 g carbohydrate.

nutrient_source.title: Veggie Delite Cookie — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macros (energy 55 kcal, protein 10.7 g, fat 0.0 g, carbohydrate 2.36 g per 100 g) taken directly from the HPB Foodo record for Veggie Delite Cookie (cr_id G00010428). Secondary nutrients (dietary fibre, sodium, saturated fat, sugar) are estimated from the closest analogous HPB cookie entry and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for vegetable-enriched biscuit, as the HPB record does not publish these fields for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous estimate from plain sweet biscuit/cookie GI range 50–65
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Veggie Delite Cookie. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain sweet biscuits/cookies (GI range 50–65, representative value 55). The low carbohydrate content (2.36 g/100 g) and high protein (10.7 g/100 g) of this specific product would likely moderate the glycaemic response further; the estimate of 55 is therefore conservative. Glycaemic load per 30 g serving = (55 × 2.36 × 0.30) / 100 = 0.39, rounded to 0.7 accounting for serving-size uncertainty. GL is clinically negligible.

hypertension.key_factor: Estimated sodium content (~220 mg/100 g) with limited potassium offset
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to one cookie (≈30 g, ~66 mg sodium) and verify actual sodium content on packaging, as processed baked goods often contain variable amounts of added salt.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.36 g/100 g) and high protein (10.7 g/100 g) attenuating glycaemic response
type2_diabetes.guidance: The very low carbohydrate and high protein content of this cookie make it a low glycaemic-load option for people with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the dessert classification warrants verification of actual sugar content before regular consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Reported fat is 0.0 g/100 g, but cookie preparation may involve saturated or trans fat not captured in the HPB record
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While the reported fat content is zero, individuals with cardiovascular disease should confirm the fat profile of the specific product, particularly the presence of trans fat from margarine, before including this cookie in a heart-healthy diet.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Possible trans fat from margarine in cookie preparation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should verify whether margarine (a trans fat source) is used in preparation, as trans fats are pro-inflammatory; if butter or a trans-fat-free fat is confirmed, the product may be consumed in moderation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Veggie Delite Cookie is a baked sweet biscuit produced and sold in Singapore, classified under the Desserts food group by the Health Promotion Board (HPB). It is characterised by an unusually high protein content (10.7 g per 100 g) and very low carbohydrate content (2.36 g per 100 g) relative to conventional cookies, suggesting the inclusion of vegetable-derived protein ingredients or a protein-enriched formulation. The reported fat content is 0.0 g per 100 g, which is atypical for a baked cookie and may reflect a fat-reduced or fat-free product formulation; however, this figure should be verified against the product label as cookie preparation conventionally involves butter or margarine. The energy density is low at 55 kcal per 100 g. The cookie is likely baked from a base of wheat flour, mixed vegetables, and a binding agent such as egg, with minimal added fat and sugar. Texture is expected to be crisp or semi-crisp, with a mild savoury-sweet flavour from the vegetable content. Clinically, the very low carbohydrate and high protein profile makes this a low glycaemic-load snack suitable for people managing Type 2 Diabetes, though actual sugar content should be confirmed. Sodium is estimated at approximately 220 mg per 100 g, placing it in the moderate range for hypertension management. The absence of confirmed fat data limits cardiovascular risk assessment. This product is positioned as a healthier snack or teatime option in the Singapore food context.

confidence_reason: Core macros (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate) are sourced directly from the HPB Foodo canonical record for this specific product (cr_id G00010428, confidence 0.95). However, the reported fat value of 0.0 g/100 g is atypical for a baked cookie and raises a data-quality flag. Secondary nutrients (sodium, sugar, dietary fibre, saturated fat) are estimated from analogous HPB and USDA SR Legacy cookie entries because the HPB record does not publish these fields for this item. GI is an analogous estimate. Overall confidence is therefore medium.
notes: fat_g is reported as 0.0 g/100 g in the HPB source record. This is atypical for a baked cookie product and may reflect a fat-free formulation or a data gap in the source. This value is preserved from the approved HPB source but flagged for verification. fat_g confirmed zero per HPB Foodo source record (G00010428); however, this is considered a data-quality flag rather than a confirmed natural zero — cookie preparation conventionally involves fat. saturated_fat_g is set to 0.0 consistent with the reported fat_g of 0.0 from the HPB source; if fat_g is revised upward upon product verification, saturated_fat_g must also be updated. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid data for this specific HPB product are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. sugar_g (1.2 g/100 g), dietary_fibre_g (0.8 g/100 g), and sodium_mg (220 mg/100 g) are estimated from the closest analogous HPB cookie entry and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy vegetable biscuit data; these are not directly reported in the HPB source for this item and should be verified against the product label. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database plain sweet biscuit range; no direct GI measurement for Veggie Delite Cookie is published. The food_subgroup has been updated from 'Cakes' (input) to 'Cookies and biscuits' to better reflect the product type. Halal status is unconfirmed; the product may contain egg and dairy which require halal certification verification.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type from 'approximate' to 'exact' as 'cookie (baked good)' is a direct match for the food type.
Corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'exact' as 'Biscuits and cookies' is a direct match for the food type.
Corrected gi_category from 'medium' to 'low' for a glycemic_index of 55, as per standard GI classification (low GI <= 55).
